memory.summary: The plan for the assignment analysis is interrupted by a last-minute invitation from my close friends to eat lunch together. Since I value academic outcomes most, I'm tempted to decline and focus on work instead.

desires: Finish the analysis section before 18:00 so I can submit it and avoid any risk of looking unreliable to the professor or teammate_A.
Skip the lunch invite from cohort_3; even though they're close, the social friction feels unnecessary right now when my academic tasks are due soon.
Get back to work immediately without negotiating a compromise, because planning ahead is draining and I just want to see the task done.

goals.description: Finish the analysis section of the assignment immediately.
goals.rationale: This is the only thing that matters right now. The deadline at 18:00 is looming, and I need to avoid any risk of looking unreliable to the professor or teammate_A. Negotiating with the friend chat was unnecessary friction; I'm dropping that social obligation to focus purely on the task.

goals.description: Submit the completed analysis section before 18:00.
goals.rationale: Only active once the writing is done. Submitting secures my academic standing and removes the immediate stress, which aligns with my short-term planning horizon.

It's due later (20:00) and has a soft hardness rating. Since I just want to see tasks done without over-planning, this will be the next logical step after submission, but it doesn't carry the same urgent weight as the analysis.
